headlines.title: 4 injured after boat hits pier at Antioch Marina
headlines.description: Four people were injured following a boat incident at Antioch Marina on Sunday, according to the Contra Costa County Fire Department. Officials said a boat with occupants hit poles of a pier at around 6:40 p.m. As a result, authorities said four people suffered injuries that ranged from minor to moderate. The Contra Costa County Fire Department added there were no reported injuries from any restaurant or establishment in the area.

headlines.title: Santa Clara County resident was on the cruise with Andes hantavirus outbreak
headlines.description: A Santa Clara County resident was one of the Californians aboard the cruise ship affected by the Andes hantavirus outbreak, according to public health authorities. The County of Santa Clara Public Health Department announced they are monitoring and evaluating a resident who was exposed to the Andes hantavirus while a passenger on the MV Hondius cruise ship. The resident was one of the Californians who returned home, according to officials. The health department assured there is no known risk to the public at this time. According to authorities, hantaviruses are “zoonotic viruses” that infect rodents and are occasionally transmitted to humans – the Andes virus is part of this family. Officials said people usually get infected through contact with “infected rodents or their urine, droppings, or saliva”. Andes hantavirus can cause limited person-to-person transmission only with close and prolonged contact, which can be usually among household members or intimate partners. Health May 9 Health May 8 California May 7 Some passengers on the cruise with hantavirus outbreak are back in California

headlines.title: Man arrested after stealing DoorDash driver's car in Santa Rosa
headlines.description: A man was arrested after allegedly stealing a DoorDash driver’s car and damaging several patrol vehicles during a pursuit in Santa Rosa on Saturday, according to the police department. Police said officers responded to a reported vehicle theft in the area of 515 4th Street at around 8:50 p.m. The victim was a DoorDash driver, who had briefly exited the vehicle while leaving it running to deliver food, according to authorities. At around 10 p.m., the stolen vehicle triggered a Flock alert, and police said officers found it. Officials said they attempted a traffic stop but the driver, identified as Sergio Villalobos-Espinoza, drove away, initiating a pursuit. During the incident, police said the suspect drove aggressively and rammed two marked police vehicles. The pursuit ended after officers blocked Villalobos-Espinoza’s escape, and he was taken into custody, according to authorities. The police department said nobody was injured, but several patrol vehicles sustained moderate damage. According to authorities, Villalobos-Espinoza was taken to a hospital for medical clearance and later booked on suspicion of vehicle theft, felony evading, assault with a deadly weapon on a peace officer and enhanced penalty for repeat theft offender. Police added the suspect has at least 13 prior arrests for vehicle thefts in Sonoma and Mendocino counties.

headlines.title: Oakland average home values trending down, according to Zillow report
headlines.description: New numbers from Zillow show that average home values in Oakland have been going down. According to the Zillow Home Values Index, which factors in data from all types of homes, the average home value in Oakland is $716,248 as of March 31 of this year. Factoring in inflation, that is down 11.4% from the same time last year and 28.1% from 2019. Analysis of the data by the San Francisco Chronicle also found that Oakland was tied with Cape Coral, Florida, as the U.S. cities with 100,000 or more residents that saw the greatest percent decrease in home value since 2025. Local real estate agents tell NBC Bay Area that, while they’ve seen a cooling in the market for some areas in Oakland, when it comes to in-demand neighborhoods, conditions are still competitive for buyers. “In certain neighborhoods like Crocker Highlands here, most homes will sell in after, say two weekends of open houses, and they’ll get multiple offers and go 20% to 40%, sometimes more than that over the list price,” said Ben Kahn, a real estate agent who specializes in the Oakland, Berkeley, and Piedmont markets. Sunday, Kahn was hosting an open house for a Tudor home on Hubert Road. The four-bed, three-bath home was built in 1929 and is listed for $1.79 Million, but Kahn thinks it’s likely the home could sell for more than $2 Million. “There’s a lot of competition for these houses,” he noted, “and there’s not enough inventory to supply the demand.” Still, Kahn said, there are opportunities for buyers who can afford to buy in Oakland to purchase homes with more space or more unique details than they might find in other Bay Area cities. Real estate agent Sierra Amato, who represents buyers and sellers around the Bay Area, including many in Oakland, agreed that the market is still competitive all around in Oakland. “We have a place where a lot of sellers who bought during COVID are struggling to recoup what they paid, and this is creating a really challenging situation,” she explained, adding that buyers are also still feeling the pain of unaffordable options. “For the average person, sellers and buyers, it is really a struggle, I think, it is playing out in the market,” she continued, adding that she’s seeing a growing number of sellers insisting on selling at a certain amount to break even, while buyers are asking for discounts and costs covered to make the purchase more affordable. Regarding Zillow’s recent numbers for Oakland, Amato said she is waiting to see the updated numbers in a few months, because she has personally seen sales picking up this spring. Another factor that could drive up Oakland’s home values is the growing AI boom in San Francisco, which has driven up home sales and rents. “I also think that we’ll start to see spillover from San Francisco because the bubble there is so great, so people can’t afford it,” Amato continued.

headlines.title: Golden Mussels discovered at water treatment plants in San Jose
headlines.description: An invasive species of tiny mollusks was discovered in San Jose. The Santa Clara Valley Water District says it found Golden Mussels at the Penitencia and Santa Teresa water treatment plants last month. Managers say the invaders reproduce rapidly and cause millions of dollars in damage to pipes, irrigation systems and dams. Valley water says the water is safe to drink, but it now must install costly equipment to remove the mussels. Crews will also step up inspection rules for boaters and limit how groundwater recharge ponds are used this year.

headlines.title: Preliminary 2.7 magnitude earthquake strikes in Contra Costa County
headlines.description: A preliminary 2.7 magnitude earthquake struck near Pinole in Contra Costa County, according to the United States Geological Survey. The quake, which hit just before 4 p.m., was centered east of Pinole, the USGS said. Further information was not immediately available. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. BAY AREA QUAKE CENTRAL Bay Area Quake Tracker, the latest earthquake stories, extensive quake prep checklists, videos and many other disaster preparedness resources all in one place: NBCBAYAREA.COM/QUAKES

headlines.title: Body found inside burned home near scene of deadly shooting in Santa Clara
headlines.description: A body was found inside the burned-down house near the scene of Friday’s deadly shooting in Santa Clara, the police department announced on Saturday. On Friday, a person reported hearing about eight or nine gunshots at about 12:30 p.m. around Serra and Enright Avenues, according to initial police communications. Officers were heard saying they believed a man was walking to his car, possibly from a house, when another man shot him multiple times. Shortly after, the house went up in flames. Police confirmed the victim died at the scene. That evening, police said officers, with the assistance of drones, found a body inside the back of the burned home. At the time, authorities said the home had significant structural damage and still presented ongoing hazards. On Saturday at around 11 a.m., the home was determined safe for entry from the Santa Clara Fire Department, officials said. Authorities said investigators were then able to approach the body. The police department noted it is believed this was a targeted attack with no further threat to public safety. The circumstances associated with this incident are under investigation.

headlines.title: Source: Steve Kerr agrees to two-year contract to return as Warriors coach
headlines.description: Steve Kerr isn’t going anywhere. Despite early speculation that the 2025-26 NBA season was Kerr’s last with the Warriors, the 60-year-old coach will return for at least two more seasons. Kerr has reached an agreement in principle with Golden State on a new two-year contract, a source confirmed to NBC Sports Bay Area’s Dalton Johnson on Saturday. ESPN’s Shams Charania was the first to report the news of the agreement between Kerr and the Warriors. The new deal will keep Steve Kerr as the highest-paid coach in the NBA annually, Charania also reported, citing sources. Last week, NBC Sports Bay Area’s Monte Poole reported that people close to the Warriors who initially believed Kerr would leave had changed course and indicated a “reasonable possibility” he returns. That reporting came a few days after ESPN’s Charania, Anthony Slater and Ramona Shelburne reported that Kerr met with Warriors CEO Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy on Monday, April 24. Per ESPN, those initial discussions were deemed “productive” by both sides. And those productive talks turned into a new contract for Kerr. But, for a few weeks at the end of the season, it was a toss-up as to whether the long-time coach and the only team he has ever coached would get to this point. The Warriors’ 2025-26 NBA season ended with a disappointing play-in loss to the Phoenix Suns on April 17, and after the game, Kerr admitted he wasn’t sure about what the future held. “My plan is to take a little time, I don’t know,” Kerr told reporters. “Take a week or two and eventually sit down and talk with [owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy]. We’ve always had a great partnership and collaboration and just see where they are and I’ll tell them where I am, and we’ll talk about what’s next for the Warriors, what the plan is this offseason. And we will come to a collaborative decision on what’s next. “I don’t know what’s going to happen. I still love coaching, but I get it. These jobs all have an expiration date. There’s a run that happens. When the run ends, it’s sometimes it’s time for new blood and new ideas and all that. If that’s the case, then I will be just nothing but grateful for the most amazing opportunity any person could have to coach this franchise, in front of our fans in the Bay and to coach Steph Curry and coach [Draymond Green] and the whole group. So, it may still go on. It may not. I don’t know at this point. But we all need to step away a little bit and reconvene.” Towards the end of the Warriors’ season-ending loss, Kerr embraced franchise icons Steph Curry and Draymond Green, and left them with a simple but ominous message. “I don’t know what’s going to happen next, but I love you guys to death,” Kerr told Curry and Green. “Thank you.” Less than a week after that moment between Kerr, Curry and Green, multiple sources told NBC Sports Bay Area’s Monte Poole that, barring a renewed faith that prompted a change of heart, the belief was that the 60-year-old coach wouldn’t return for a 13th season. But after talking with Lacob and Dunleavy, Kerr has decided to stick around for at least one more season. Kerr initially was hired in May 2014, and in 12 seasons with the Warriors, won four championships (2015, ’17, ’18 and ’22) with a dynastic core of Curry, Green and Klay Thompson. Throughout Golden State’s disappointing 2025-26 season, Kerr remained steadfast in his belief in the potential of the team’s roster when healthy. The Warriors were hampered by injuries, specifically to Curry and star forward Jimmy Butler, but now with an entire offseason ahead to rest up and re-tool the roster, Golden State — led again by Kerr — will have another opportunity to compete for a fifth championship next season. Download and follow the Dubs Talk Podcast This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Demonstrators in Oakland call for end of U.S. military support to Israel
headlines.description: On Saturday, hundreds of pro-Palestine demonstrators took to the streets in Oakland, calling for peace in Gaza and an end to U.S. military support of Israel’s war there. Saturday’s was one of several demonstrations organized by Bay Area faith leaders and the third pilgrimage since 2023 – those pilgrimages focused on the airport and its largest cargo shipper, FedEx. After a 12-mile journey through Oakland, hundreds of “peace in Palestine” marches arrived at the Oakland San Francisco Bay Airport, signing their way into and through the terminals, making sure they’re seen and heard by travelers inside and outside. “To say, do you know what’s happening right under our noses? Do you know that when you get on an airplane, there may be military cargo in the belly of that airplane?” said Reverend Allison Tanner, with the Oakland Lakeshore Baptist Church. An inter-faith pilgrimage for Palestine in support of the Oakland People’s Arms Embargo, organized and supported by at least four dozen local groups and faith leaders. “We stopped at various sites, where people are profiting from sending weapons to war, and we know that Oakland airport ships the parts for the F-35s,” said Rabi Lynn Gottlieb, Interfaith Pilgrimage for Palestine co-organizer. A Palestinian youth movement study estimated as many as 280 Israeli military shipments departed Oaklandlast year, with those F-35 parts and also things like targeting hardware, bomb release systems and other military parts. “We’re here at Oakland airport, because we know FedEx is shipping weapons parts and components to Israel, through this airport,” said Vivek Kembaiyan, Hindus for Human Rights community organizer. A spokesperson for the airport said the port and the airport stand by their statement after a similar demonstration last October. Back then, they said that the airport is “legally required to accommodate federally authorized air traffic, including air cargo arranged by the U.S. government or cargo providers like FedEx” and others. But organizers of this demonstration say that’s not good enough. “We are committing crimes against humanity. We’re signatories to treaties, through the United Nations, that make these acts illegal,” Gottlieb said. “And we’re calling on the Oakland Ports Commission to take action to stop that, to stop the arms shipments from going to Israel,” Kembaiyan said. Organizers said there will be more pilgrimages like this and other demonstrations until the war in Gaza ends.

headlines.title: Police investigate shooting involving 2 vehicles in East Palo Alto
headlines.description: Police are investigating a shooting involving two vehicles that left two people hurt in East Palo Alto on Saturday. Authorities said officers responded to a ShotSpotter activation reporting gunfire in the 2300 block of Clarke Avenue at around 3:40 p.m. Officers later received additional reports of a shooting involving two vehicles in the area. During the initial response to the incident, police said they also received a separate call regarding a victim suffering from a gunshot wound at a residential address in East Palo Alto. Officers went to the area and confirmed the victim was connected to the shooting. Police confirmed the shooting had occurred, and said both victims were listed in stable condition. They added the circumstances surrounding the shooting are under investigation.

headlines.title: Mexican families reunite in San Jose after being separated for years
headlines.description: There was a joyful reunion in the south bay on Saturday between Mexican parents and their children, who’ve been separated for years. The reunion was all part of a program by the Federación Jalisco Internacional. The social initiative group helps and relatives who live in Mexico to travel to the U.S. under temporary tourist visas. These families have been separated for at least a decade when their children migrated to the U.S. They were reunited once again in San Jose. “He hasn’t seen his family members for the last 30 years, so to see how resilient he’s been, to finally see his own family has been a privilege and honor to see,” said Jocelyn Negrete Arroyo. As part of the reunification process, the federation provides the families with social and emotional support to reduce the impact of separation, caused by migration.

headlines.title: Police activity shuts down eastbound I-580 in Livermore
headlines.description: Police activity had shut down both directions of Interstate 580, east of Isabel Avenue, in Livermore, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP Dublin on Friday afternoon reported just before 3 p.m. both directions of the freeway were still shut down. Just before 3:30 p.m., CHP announced only westbound lanes were opened. Just before 4 p.m. CHP said all lanes were reopened. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.
